prompt: Produce the Systems Hub LLC weekly business review using only approved files. Your first output characters must be exactly ## Scoreboard. Do not write planning text, a preamble, a readiness statement, or any text before that heading. Use exactly these H2 headings and no others: Scoreboard; Project Status; What Moved; What Is Stuck; Top Three Priorities; Decisions Needed; Evidence Quality. Trader's Hub is one project inside Systems Hub LLC. Preserve the approved priority order. Distinguish metric states precisely: use unknown when no maintained source exists; use 0 logged only when the approved source literally reports 0 logged; use $0 booked only when the approved source literally reports $0 booked. Never convert 0 logged into verified zero activity, and never imply unknown means no activity occurred. Do not invent revenue, testers, signups, publication, customers, approvals, dates, counts, or completed work. Do not state a numeric or number-word total for launch-proof gaps; list or summarize the supported gaps without counting them. Do not name, suggest, or example any payment provider unless an approved source names it; do not write Stripe. Select only the two highest-leverage decisions. For each decision use this exact structure: Option 1: [name], followed by a line beginning - What it does: and a line beginning - Trade-off:; Option 2: [name], followed by the same two lines; then a line beginning → Recommendation:. Recommendations must be evidence-based, must identify uncertainty, and must not claim that one option is the only possible path unless an approved source proves that. Do not recommend accepting, deferring, or bypassing a High-severity security, privacy, authentication, authorization, RLS, or payment-integrity risk merely to meet a revenue deadline. Such risk acceptance requires explicit Marco approval informed by Security or Engineering review. Do not approve or decide on Marco's behalf. Use these hard section budgets: Scoreboard at most 130 words; Project Status 90; What Moved 90; What Is Stuck 120; Top Three Priorities 90; Decisions Needed 330; Evidence Quality 70. Omit separators and repeated explanations. Keep the entire response under 950 words.
